Original question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Draft answer:
FINAL_ANSWER: The problem requires constructing a bitstring using a Trie-based approach or Aho-Corasick to ensure no reported ID is a prefix of another. 
JUSTIFICATION: Use a Trie to manage prefixes and ensure unique identification.

authored_response.response:
FINAL_ANSWER: Construct a bitstring by greedily appending 0 or 1 to a Trie-based structure, ensuring no existing ID is a prefix of the new ID and no new ID is a prefix of existing ones.
JUSTIFICATION: Greedy Trie construction ensures prefix-free property and optimal length.
